executiveSummary: A platform establishing a reliable pipeline to traverse design graphs, map metadata to active component atlases, and autonomously generate production-ready frontend components, ensuring strict parity between UX intent and deployed code.

systemContext.description: The platform sits between the external design tool ecosystem and our internal CI/CD repositories.
systemContext.systemBoundary: The system encapsulates the webhook listener, the orchestration harness, and the code generation engine. External design APIs and GitHub are outside the boundary.

majorSystems.name: Design Graph Ingestion Service
coreResponsibilities: Listen for external design publish webhooks
Normalize metadata into a standard intermediate graph format

majorSystems.name: Agentic Execution Harness
coreResponsibilities: Provide defensive guardrails for AI agent execution
Route normalized data to specialized generation skills

majorSystems.name: Polyglot Generation Engine
coreResponsibilities: Map intermediate nodes to React component ASTs
Generate Fastify/FastAPI boilerplate for required backend endpoints

dataFlows.name: Component Publish Pipeline
dataFlows.description: Triggered when a designer publishes a new library component, flowing through to a pull request.

computeTier: EC2 Auto Scaling Groups (for intensive agent execution harnesses)

nonFunctionalRequirements.scalability: Must handle concurrent processing of up to 500 design graph updates per minute.
nonFunctionalRequirements.availability: 99.9% uptime during standard business hours; system can degrade to a queued state during ingestion API outages.
nonFunctionalRequirements.latency: End-to-end processing from webhook receipt to PR generation must be under 3 minutes for libraries < 50 components.
security: Strict secret scanning applied to all generated ASTs prior to PR creation
AWS Identity Center RBAC for all internal service-to-service calls
